title: Classifying Cash Flows: O / I / F
event: During the year, Reyes Co. issued common stock for $50,000, purchased equipment for $30,000, paid dividends of $10,000, and collected $90,000 from customers. Compute net cash from financing activities and classify each item.

computationPaths.narration: Operating = day-to-day earnings engine. Investing = long-term assets. Financing = deals with owners and lenders.

memorize.body: Financing = who FUNDS you (stock, dividends, borrowings, repayments). Investing = what you BUY to operate long-term. Operating = everything the income statement is made of.
Dividends PAID are financing; interest paid is operating under GAAP — a favorite exam distinction.

questions.prompt: Stock issued $50,000, equipment bought $30,000, dividends paid $10,000. Net FINANCING cash flow = ?

distractors.feedback: Dividends are an OUTFLOW of financing: 50,000 − 10,000 = 40,000.
You pulled the equipment purchase into financing. Buying long-term assets is INVESTING: financing is 50,000 − 10,000.
